steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Text 2's author explains why Créte and Jolicoeur's moose study found no predation effect while other predation relief studies do show prey increases. The key must come from Text 2's own argument.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — prey size and reproduction rate: Text 2 explicitly argues that other studies use small, rapidly reproducing prey (like mice), while moose are large and slow-reproducing — that difference drives the divergent results. ✓
  • Choice B — predator size ratios: Text 2 does not discuss predator size ratios; this is introduced nowhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — feral cats vs. wolves: Text 2 does not compare feral cats to wolves; this comparison is absent. ✗
  • Choice D — contradicts Text 2: Text 2 explicitly states that many predation relief studies show similar increases in various small prey, so D contradicts the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
